author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
gāite gāite nāma ki daśā hoila
‘kṛṣṇa nityadāsa muñi’ hṛdaye sphurila

jānilāma māyāpāśe e⋅jaḍa jagate
govinda⋅virahe duḥkha pāi nānā mate

āra je saṁsāra mora nāhi lāge bhāla
kā̐hā jāi, kṛṣṇa heri⋅e cintā viśāla

kā̐dite kā̐dite mora ā̐khi variṣaya
varṣādhārā hena cakṣe hoila udaya

nimeṣa hoila mora śatayuga⋅sama
govinda⋅viraha āra sahite akṣama

śūnya dharātala, caudike dekhiye,
parāṇa udāsa haya
ki kari, ki kari, sthira nāhi haya,
jīvana nāhika raya

vrajavāsigaṇa, mora prāṇa rākha,
dekhāo śrī⋅rādhānāthe
bhakativinoda⋅, minati māniyā,
loohe tāhāre sāthe

śrī⋅kṛṣṇaviraha āra sahite nā pāri
parāṇa chāḍite āra dina dui cāri
en_translation:
As I sang the Holy Name, what a state I reached! "I am Kṛṣṇa's eternal servant" manifested in my heart.

I realized that in this material world, bound by illusion, I suffer in various ways due to separation from Govinda.

This worldly life no longer seems good to me. "Where shall I go to see Kṛṣṇa?"—this thought is vast.

As I weep, my eyes pour down a rain-like torrent.

A moment has become like a hundred eons for me. I am no longer able to bear separation from Govinda.

The earth is empty; looking all around, my heart becomes indifferent. What shall I do? I cannot remain steady, and my life itself seems to depart.

O residents of Vraja, save my life! Show me the Lord of Rādhā. Bhaktivinoda makes this humble request: please take him along with you.

I can no longer tolerate separation from Śrī Kṛṣṇa. My life will depart in just a few days.
